description: Build an AI-driven browser automation platform integrating Browser-use and Stagehand with a unified interface.

userStories.title: Unified Automation Interface
userStories.description: Define a core architecture and unified TypeScript interface that abstracts different browser automation engines (Browser-use, Stagehand, etc.).
acceptanceCriteria: Create 'AutomationEngine' interface with methods like navigate(), click(), type(), and screenshot()
Implement a factory or registry to load specific engine wrappers
Define common error types for automation failures
Unit test: interface methods are correctly defined and typed
userStories.technicalNotes: Use TypeScript interfaces. Ensure abstraction is generic enough for both DOM-based and vision-based engines.

userStories.title: Browser-use Engine Wrapper
userStories.description: Implement a wrapper for the Browser-use Python library, allowing it to be controlled through the unified interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Bridge TypeScript calls to Python 'browser-use' (via shell/IPC or a lightweight API)
Handle session state and screenshot capture from Browser-use
Verify basic navigation and click operations through the wrapper
userStories.technicalNotes: Browser-use is Python-based; implemented via a FastAPI bridge server managed by a PythonBridgeEngine base class.

userStories.title: Stagehand Engine Wrapper
userStories.description: Implement a wrapper for the Stagehand (Playwright-based) framework, integrating it into the unified interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Initialize Stagehand/Playwright context and handle page lifecycle
Implement core actions (click, type, navigate) using Stagehand's high-level API
Verify reliable selector-based or AI-based actions via Stagehand
userStories.technicalNotes: Stagehand is Node.js-native, integrated directly using its Playwright-based API.

userStories.title: Natural Language CLI Entry Point
userStories.description: Create a basic CLI that accepts natural language commands and routes them to the appropriate engine.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI tool 'ai-vision' that takes a text prompt as input
Basic routing logic to select an engine (or default to Browser-use)
Log execution steps to the console and to a local SQLite database
Basic session summary output after task completion
userStories.technicalNotes: Uses 'commander' and 'node:sqlite' (built-in Node 24 feature) for history tracking.

userStories.title: Remove Stagehand engine
acceptance: src/engines/stagehand/ directory deleted
StagehandEngine removed from registry and exports
No stagehand references remain in src/
npx tsc --noEmit passes
npm test passes
npm run build passes

userStories.title: YAML workflow loader
acceptance: workflows/*.yaml directory exists with at least post_to_reddit.yaml
src/orchestrator/loader.ts loads and validates YAML against zod schema
CLI workflow command accepts a .yaml file path in addition to built-in names
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: Markdown instruction loader
acceptance: instructions/orchestrator.md exists
instructions/author.md exists
instructions/executor.md exists
src/orchestrator/loader.ts reads and returns instruction markdown
Content is injected into Claude context at session start

userStories.title: New MCP tools: write_copy, query_telemetry, read_memory, write_memory
acceptance: mcp/server.ts exposes write_copy tool calling GeminiWriter
mcp/server.ts exposes query_telemetry tool reading telemetry DB
mcp/server.ts exposes read_memory and write_memory tools
mcp/server.ts exposes list_workflows tool listing workflows/*.yaml
All tools have zod-validated input schemas
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: Claude orchestrator loop
acceptance: src/orchestrator/loop.ts implements Claude tool-calling agent loop
Loop reads YAML workflow + md instructions before first tool call
Loop reads memory bank context before first tool call
Loop enforces permissions.require_human_approval_before from YAML
workflowEngine.run() delegates to orchestrator loop when YAML source
End-to-end: workflow write_and_post_to_reddit completes via Claude loop
npx tsc --noEmit passes
npm test passes

userStories.title: Memory bank seeding
acceptance: memory/bank/ directory created
memory/bank/world.md seeded with known platform quirks from session history
memory/bank/platform/reddit.md seeded with r/test and r/artificial observations
memory/bank/user_preferences.md created with defaults
bank-reader.ts reads and formats bank files for Claude context injection

userStories.title: browser-use live event bridge
acceptance: browser-use Python server POSTs action events to BROWSER_USE_CALLBACK_URL
Node.js python-bridge receives and emits telemetry for each browser-use action
UI WebSocket forwards browser_use.action.* events to client in real-time
Orchestrator loop receives mid-task browser state without polling
npx tsc --noEmit passes
npm test passes

userStories.title: Webhooks — inbound triggers and outbound notifications
acceptance: src/webhooks/server.ts exposes POST /webhooks/trigger accepting {workflow_id, params}
Inbound request validates signature (WEBHOOK_SECRET) before executing workflow
Workflow result POSTed to configured callback_url if provided in trigger payload
Outbound POST includes {workflow_id, success, outputs, durationMs, timestamp}
webhook_secret stored via vault:export, never in plaintext env
src/webhooks/server.ts registers with Express/Fastify app on AI_VISION_WEBHOOK_PORT
npx tsc --noEmit passes
npm test passes

userStories.title: TSC crash diagnostic classification baseline
userStories.description: Create a reproducible FORGE investigation path for `tsc --noEmit` memory crashes that distinguishes Node/V8 heap aborts from kernel OOM kills and checker-graph pathologies.
acceptance: Investigation flow records local Node, pnpm, TypeScript, and `NODE_OPTIONS` before reproducing the crash
Investigation compares local behavior against the CI-pinned Node 24 baseline before blaming the TypeScript graph
Investigation captures V8 abort evidence, file-count shape, and extended diagnostics values including `Instantiations`, `Memory used`, and `Check time`
Investigation captures Linux OOM evidence from `dmesg`, `free -h`, `ulimit -a`, and cgroup memory files
Final report classifies the failure as OS/kernel, Node/V8 runtime, or TypeScript checker path with evidence for the ruled-in and ruled-out layers

Story is complete only when a bug report artifact and YAML refactoring artifact both exist, record the observed crash signature, runtime baseline, Node 24 comparison result, layer-by-layer evidence, graphed metrics, and clear refactoring procedures for the ruled-in bug path
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when `docs/artifacts/tsc-crash-forge-story.yaml` authors the story with layers, shapes, graphed data, and bug-refactoring procedures, and the paired bug report artifact records a final ruled-in or ruled-out classification across OS/kernel, Node/V8, and TypeScript checker layers.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means the investigation yields an explicit error or bug report with concise refactoring procedures, names the ruled-in layer, rules out the competing layers with evidence, and persists the result in the FORGE YAML story artifact plus a human-readable report.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from `docs/artifacts/tsc_crash_error_investigation.md`. Preserve the `SIGABRT` versus `SIGKILL` distinction, treat Node 24 parity as the first discriminator, and use `docs/artifacts/tsc-crash-forge-story.yaml` as the structured story-authoring artifact.

userStories.title: TSC crash layered remediation protocol
userStories.description: Convert the tsc crash investigation output into an explicit remediation workflow that gates runtime parity first, separates Node/V8 remediation from OS remediation, delays TypeScript refactors until upstream layers are ruled out, and persists the result as a classified bug report.
acceptance: Remediation protocol records local Node, pnpm, TypeScript, and `NODE_OPTIONS` and treats CI-pinned Node 24 parity as the first remediation gate
Remediation protocol separates `SIGABRT` Node/V8 evidence from `SIGKILL` or kernel/cgroup evidence and routes them to different remediation paths
TypeScript refactoring is blocked until Node 24 parity and OS kill-path checks are ruled out with evidence
Remediation result persists as a classified bug report that names the ruled-in layer, rules out competing layers, and provides concise refactoring procedures

Story is complete only when a YAML remediation story artifact and a paired bug report remediation artifact both exist and record layers, shapes, graphed metrics, and remediation procedures for the ruled-in path
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when `docs/artifacts/tsc-crash-remediation-forge-story.yaml` and its paired remediation bug report artifact both exist, runtime parity has been checked or explicitly documented as unavailable, and the final report names the ruled-in remediation layer with concise procedures and ruled-out competing layers.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means the remediation workflow produces an explicit error or bug report that identifies whether the active remediation path is OS/kernel, Node/V8 runtime, or TypeScript checker, and persists the required refactoring procedures in both a human-readable report and a FORGE YAML story artifact.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from the consolidated remediation requirements in `docs/artifacts/ts-memory-troubleshooting.md`, `docs/artifacts/tsc_crash_error_investigation.md`, `docs/artifacts/tsc-crash-bug-report.md`, and `docs/artifacts/tsc-crash-forge-story.yaml`. Preserve the layered order: runtime parity, OS kill-path elimination, then TypeScript refactoring.

userStories.title: Direct workflow gate layer design
userStories.description: Design the direct workflow gate layer that makes the TypeScript engine the authoritative workflow kernel for skip logic, approval enforcement, HITL publication, side-effect policy, and output validation, without deleting `mode: agentic` yet.
acceptance: Produce a gate-layer design artifact that defines `GateDecision`, `GateContext`, and the trace/state surfaces required by the direct engine
Map the exact gate insertion points in `src/workflow/engine.ts` for before-step, in-step, post-step, HITL, side-effect, and wrap-up boundaries
Define the canonical HITL state publication model across `hitlCoordinator`, `workflowEngine.currentState`, `/api/status`, and websocket projection
Define how approval and side-effect policies block Python/browser-use execution before irreversible browser mutations
Define the direct-path validation rules for empty, placeholder, unresolved, and context-invalid generated outputs
Define the minimum test matrix required before `mode: agentic` can be quarantined or retired
Do not implement the gate layer yet and do not remove `mode: agentic` in this story
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when the repo contains a Forge storyline, YAML story card, implementation handoff, and explicit definition-of-done for the direct workflow gate layer design, and the design package clearly identifies the future gate interfaces, insertion points, state owners, and test map without changing runtime behavior.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means the direct workflow gate layer has a governed design package that is specific enough for a later implementation story to execute without re-discovering the architecture, and the package explicitly keeps TypeScript as the workflow kernel, Python as the bounded intelligence layer, Rust out of scope, and `mode: agentic` still present until direct-path gates are proven.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from `docs/architecture/as-built_execution_atlas.md` and `docs/artifacts/Discovery_mode-agentic.md`. Keep the story design-only: interface and placement first, implementation later.

userStories.title: Direct workflow gate layer implementation - Phase 1 HITL state publication
userStories.description: Implement the first direct-path gate-layer slice by making the TypeScript workflow engine publish every direct HITL wait and terminal transition through one canonical public state path, without removing `mode: agentic` or widening into approval and side-effect enforcement yet.
acceptance: Implement a canonical state publication helper in `src/workflow/engine.ts` for direct-path running, wait, complete, and error transitions
Every direct HITL wait publishes a visible operator state (`phase` plus `hitlAction`) before the blocking wait begins
`workflowEngine.currentState` remains the canonical public workflow projection while `hitlCoordinator` remains the blocking wait owner
`/api/status` and websocket/UI projection reflect the same converged direct-engine state for every direct wait and terminal transition
Regression tests cover `awaiting_human`, `pii_wait`, `hitl_qa`, final confirmation, and terminal `complete` / `error` visibility
`mode: agentic` remains present and untouched in this story
`pnpm run typecheck` passes
`pnpm test` passes
userStories.exitCriteria: Exit only when the direct engine publishes all direct HITL waits and terminal transitions through one canonical visible state path, the corresponding regression tests pass, and the story leaves approval, side-effect, and skip-gate implementation for later work.
userStories.definitionOfDone: Done means the direct workflow engine has a working Phase 1 gate-layer implementation for canonical HITL/public-state publication, operators can see every human-action state reliably through the existing UI/status surfaces, and the repo remains on the current strategic path: TypeScript kernel, Python bounded worker, `mode: agentic` not yet retired.
userStories.technicalNotes: Derived from `docs/artifacts/2026-04-24-direct-workflow-gate-layer-design.md`, especially the state ownership and `publishStateTransition()` sections. Keep this story narrow: implement state publication only, not approval or side-effect enforcement.
